title: Wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin on authenticated endpoint
description: CORS configured with wildcard origin (*) on endpoints that handle authenticated requests. This allows any website to make cross-origin requests to the API.

remediation.description: Configure CORS with a specific allowlist of trusted origins instead of using wildcard.

title: Credentials allowed with wildcard or reflected origin
description: CORS configuration allows credentials (cookies, authorization headers) with wildcard origin or origin reflection. This enables any website to make authenticated requests on behalf of the user.

remediation.description: Never combine Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true with wildcard or reflected origin. Use a strict allowlist.

title: Null origin allowed in CORS configuration
description: CORS configuration allows the null origin, which can be exploited via sandboxed iframes and redirects to bypass origin restrictions.

remediation.description: Never allow the null origin. Remove it from the origin allowlist.

title: Missing Vary: Origin header in CORS response
description: When Access-Control-Allow-Origin is set dynamically based on the request Origin, the response must include Vary: Origin to prevent cache poisoning.

remediation.description: Add Vary: Origin header when dynamically setting Access-Control-Allow-Origin based on the request.
